messages.text: how many candidates are there?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS candidate_count FROM candidates;

title: how many male candidates are there?

messages.text: how many male candidates are there?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE gender = 'male'

messages.text: how many female candidates are there?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE gender = 'female'

title: how many pget profile candidates are there?

messages.text: how many pget profile candidates are there?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE profile = 'pget' 
LIMIT 200;

messages.text: how many female pget candidates are there?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE gender = 'Female' 
  AND profile = 'PGET' 
  AND isManual = 0
LIMIT 200;

title: How many candidates are there in total?

messages.text: How many candidates are there in total?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_candidates FROM candidates;

messages.text: list the unallocated candidates?

messages.sql:
SELECT c.id, c.email, c.discipline, c.profile, c.gender, c.zone1, c.zone2, c.zone3, c.business1, c.business2, c.business3, c.meritscore
FROM candidates c
WHERE c.allocated_zone IS NULL AND c.allocated_business IS NULL AND c.allocated_ic IS NULL
LIMIT 200;

rows.discipline: NIMCAR Quantitative Surveying and Contracts Management

title: how many candidates are there from IIT colleges?

messages.text: how many candidates are there from IIT colleges?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE LOWER(institute_type) = 'iit'

messages.text: list them

messages.sql: SELECT * FROM candidates LIMIT 200;

messages.text: How many IIT candidates are unallocated?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' 
  AND allocated_ic IS NULL 
  AND allocated_zone IS NULL 
  AND allocated_business IS NULL;

messages.text: how many IIT candidates are allocated?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' 
  AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL
  AND allocated_ic != ''
LIMIT 500;

messages.text: how many IIT candidates are allocated?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' 
  AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL
  AND allocated_ic != ''
LIMIT 500;

messages.text: how many IIT candidates are not allocated?

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM candidates 
WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' 
AND allocated_ic IS NULL 
AND allocated_zone IS NULL 
AND allocated_business IS NULL
LIMIT 500;

messages.text: how many iit candidates are not allocated as pie chart

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' AND allocated_zone IS NULL AND allocated_business IS NULL AND allocated_ic IS NULL

messages.text: how many iit candidates are not allocated, show as bar chart

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' AND allocated_zone IS NULL AND allocated_business IS NULL AND allocated_ic IS NULL

messages.text: show allocated vs unallocated IIT candidates as bar chart

messages.sql:
SELECT
  c.institute_type,
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  SUM(IFNULL(c.allocated_zone, 'Unallocated')) AS allocated,
  SUM(IFNULL(c.allocated_zone, 'Unallocated')) - COUNT(*) AS unallocated
FROM
  candidates c
WHERE
  LOWER(c.institute_type) = 'iit'
GROUP BY
  c.institute_type

messages.text: show allocated vs unallocated IIT candidates as bar chart

messages.sql:
SELECT
  c.institute_type,
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  SUM(IFNULL(c.allocated_zone, 'Unallocated')) AS allocated,
  SUM(IFNULL(c.allocated_zone, 'Unallocated')) - COUNT(*) AS unallocated
FROM
  candidates c
WHERE
  LOWER(c.institute_type) = 'iit'
GROUP BY
  c.institute_type

messages.text: how many female candidates are there? and list them

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM candidates
WHERE gender = 'female'

messages.text: how many candidates are allocated for pget profile based on gender, as pie chart

messages.sql:
SELECT gender, COUNT(*) AS allocated_count
FROM candidates
WHERE profile = 'pget'
GROUP BY gender

title: how many male and female candidates are there, sho...

messages.text: how many male and female candidates are there, show it as bar chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates GROUP BY gender

title: how many get candidates allocated and group by gen...

messages.text: how many get candidates allocated and group by gender, show as bar chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE allocated_ic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender

messages.text: how many get profile candidates allocated?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE profile = 'get' AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL;

messages.text: how many male get profile candidates allocated?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE gender = 'male' AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL;

messages.text: how many male candidates allocated for GET profile?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE gender = 'male' AND allocated_ic LIKE '%GET%';

messages.text: how many Male and GET profile candidaets allocated?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE gender = 'Male' AND profile = 'GET' AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL;

title: how many candidates allocated group by gender, sho...

messages.text: how many candidates allocated group by gender, show as chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE isManual = 0 GROUP BY gender

messages.text: how many male and female candidates allocated group by get profile, show as chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, profile, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE allocated_ic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender, profile

messages.text: how many get profile canddiates are allicated, group by gernder, show as chart

messages.sql:
SELECT gender, COUNT(*) AS allocated_count
FROM candidates
WHERE profile = 'get'
AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY gender

messages.text: how many candidates allocated, show as gender report

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, gender FROM candidates WHERE allocated_ic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender

title: how many total canddiates are there

messages.text: how many total canddiates are there

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates

messages.text: how many candidates allocated, show as chart

messages.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_allocated
FROM candidates
WHERE allocated_zone IS NOT NULL AND allocated_business IS NOT NULL AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL

messages.text: how many GET profile candidates allocated and group by gender, show as chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE profile = 'GET' AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender

messages.text: how many candidates allocated and group by gender, show as chart

messages.sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE allocated_ic IS NOT NULL GROUP BY gender

messages.text: how many candiaets are allocaed and group by profile, gender show as chart

messages.sql: SELECT profile, gender, COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE allocated_zone IS NOT NULL AND allocated_business IS NOT NULL GROUP BY profile, gender

messages.text: how many IIT candidares allocated?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates WHERE institute_type = 'IIT' AND allocated_ic IS NOT NULL;

messages.text: how many candidates allocated group by instirute

messages.sql: SELECT institute_type FROM candidates GROUP BY institute_type

title: How many candidates are there in total?

messages.text: How many candidates are there in total?

messages.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM candidates;
